stimulus: As the eleventh archivist of the United States, David Ferriero held many responsibilities. These included supervising the National Archives’ holdings, which exceed more than 13 billion pages of ______blank directing the Office of Government Information Services, which mediates disputes under the Freedom of Information Act and advises agencies on transparency policy; and authenticating the daily acts of Congress.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is the punctuation of elements in a complex series. It is conventional to use a semicolon to separate items in a complex series with internal punctuation; here the semicolon after "records" separates the first item ("supervising...records") from the second ("directing...policy"), matching the semicolon used later before "and."

Choice A is incorrect because it fails to use any punctuation between the first and second series items. Choice B is incorrect because a comma after "records" does not match the semicolon used later in the series. Choice C is incorrect because it produces a sentence fragment beginning with "Directing."
